Defines a structure and functions for initializing and deinitializing a time range selection window.

# Purpose
This C header file defines the structure and functions for managing a time range selection window in a user interface. It includes necessary headers for UI components such as `SelectionLayer`, `TextLayer`, and `Window`, and it uses the `time.h` library for time-related operations. The `TimeRangeSelectionWindowData` structure contains elements for managing the window, including selection layers for the start and end times, text layers for display, and a callback function for when the selection is complete. The file declares two functions: [`time_range_selection_window_init`](<#time_range_selection_window_init>), which initializes the time range selection window with a specified color and callback, and [`time_range_selection_window_deinit`](<#time_range_selection_window_deinit>), which deinitializes the window. The callback function, `TimeRangeSelectionCompleteCallback`, is used to handle actions upon completion of the time range selection.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `applib/ui/date_time_selection_window_private.h`
- `applib/ui/selection_layer.h`
- `applib/ui/text_layer.h`
- `applib/ui/window.h`
- `time.h`


# Data Structures

---
### TimeRangeSelectionWindowData
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``window``: A `Window` object that represents the main window for the time range selection.
    - ``from_selection_layer``: A `SelectionLayer` object for selecting the start time.
    - ``to_selection_layer``: A `SelectionLayer` object for selecting the end time.
    - ``from_text_layer``: A `TextLayer` object to display the start time.
    - ``to_text_layer``: A `TextLayer` object to display the end time.
    - ``complete_callback``: A callback function that is called when the time range selection is complete.
    - ``callback_context``: A pointer to the context data for the callback function.
    - ``from``: A `TimeData` object that stores the start time.
    - ``to``: A `TimeData` object that stores the end time.
    - ``buf``: A character buffer of size 3 for temporary storage.
- **Description**: Manages the data and UI components necessary for selecting a time range within a window, including start and end time selection layers, text layers for displaying selected times, and a callback mechanism for handling completion of the selection process.


# Function Declarations (Public API)

### time\_range\_selection\_window\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:time_range_selection_window_init}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/time_range_selection_window.h#L47>)

Initializes a time range selection window.
- **Description**: Use this function to set up a time range selection window with specified visual and callback configurations. It prepares the window for user interaction by initializing selection layers for start and end times, setting their appearance, and configuring callback functions for user actions. This function must be called before the window is used, and the `time_range_selection_window` parameter must point to a valid `TimeRangeSelectionWindowData` structure.
- **Inputs**:
    - `time_range_selection_window`: A pointer to a `TimeRangeSelectionWindowData` structure that will be initialized. Must not be null.
    - `color`: A `GColor` value that sets the active background color for the selection layers.
    - `complete_callback`: A function pointer to be called when the time range selection is complete. Can be null if no callback is needed.
    - `callback_context`: A pointer to user-defined data passed to the `complete_callback`. Can be null if no context is needed.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`time_range_selection_window_init`](<time_range_selection_window.c.md#time_range_selection_window_init>)  (Implementation)

### time\_range\_selection\_window\_deinit<!-- {{#callable_declaration:time_range_selection_window_deinit}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/time_range_selection_window.h#L50>)

Deinitializes a time range selection window.
- **Description**: Use this function to deinitialize a `TimeRangeSelectionWindowData` structure when it is no longer needed. This function releases resources associated with the selection layers within the structure. It must be called to prevent resource leaks after the window is no longer in use. Ensure that the `time_range_selection_window` parameter is not null before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `time_range_selection_window`: A pointer to a `TimeRangeSelectionWindowData` structure to deinitialize. Must not be null. If null, the function does nothing.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`time_range_selection_window_deinit`](<time_range_selection_window.c.md#time_range_selection_window_deinit>)  (Implementation)
